techniques.evidence_quote: the hackers reset passwords through emails and obtained the Amazon AWS web account for our 2.0 Beta server
email account exposed imToken to our first ever security breach
an anonymous user reset our log-in password of Amazon AWS by e-mail
the hacker may have obtained certain device data of 2.0 Beta testers
the hacker may have obtained certain device data of 2.0 Beta testers

attack_chain.description: Attackers compromised an imToken corporate email account through an unspecified method (under investigation at time of disclosure).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: email account exposed imToken to our first ever security breach

evidence_chunks.quote: email account exposed imToken to our first ever security breach
email account exposed imToken to our first ever security breach

attack_chain.description: Using access to the compromised email account, attackers initiated and completed an AWS account password reset via email, changing the AWS root/login password.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: At 02:11 am 9 June 2018 (UTC+8), an anonymous user reset our log-in password of Amazon AWS by e-mail

evidence_chunks.quote: At 02:11 am 9 June 2018 (UTC+8), an anonymous user reset our log-in password of Amazon AWS by e-mail
At 02:11 am 9 June 2018 (UTC+8), an anonymous user reset our log-in password of Amazon AWS by e-mail

attack_chain.description: Attackers logged into the imToken AWS web console using the newly reset credentials, gaining access to the 2.0 Beta server environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: obtained the Amazon AWS web account for our 2.0 Beta server

evidence_chunks.quote: obtained the Amazon AWS web account for our 2.0 Beta server
obtained the Amazon AWS web account for our 2.0 Beta server

attack_chain.description: Attackers accessed 2.0 Beta server data including approximately 36,000 device IDs, 73,000 wallet addresses, and 10,000 subscribed email addresses.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the hacker may have obtained certain device data of 2.0 Beta testers

evidence_chunks.quote: the hacker may have obtained certain device data of 2.0 Beta testers
the hacker may have obtained certain device data of 2.0 Beta testers

attack_chain.description: Attackers likely exfiltrated the beta tester data (device IDs, wallet addresses, subscribed emails), enabling potential follow-on phishing campaigns against users.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: after obtaining such data, the hacker may send “Phishing emails” to users

evidence_chunks.quote: after obtaining such data, the hacker may send “Phishing emails” to users
after obtaining such data, the hacker may send “Phishing emails” to users

initial_access_vector: Compromise of imToken corporate email account (root cause under investigation), which was then used to trigger an AWS password reset via email and take over the AWS account hosting the 2.0 Beta servers.
persistence_mechanism: Reset of AWS account login password, granting attacker console access as the legitimate account owner until operations staff terminated access.
privilege_escalation: Email-based AWS password reset effectively granted attacker ownership-level privileges over the cloud account.
